comments.author: ggalimba56
comments.body:
I like the use cases in the presentation and I think we can add more UC.
A general comment is about the SDN controller definition, in y opinion should be more clear if it is clearly reported what SDN controller take a specific action. e.g. IP SDN Controller, DWDM SDN controller or Hierarchical SDN controller.

comments.author: italobusi
comments.body:
See comment from @prmanna : https://github.com/italobusi/draft-poidt-teas-actn-poi-assurance/pull/2#issuecomment-1462165159

> In general, assurance involves link failure, detect and re-route. Fabio’s slides depicts most those uses-cases in optical domain. There are PSM(protection switching module) modules can provide 50ms switchover in case of LOS. In rest of the cases, Optical PCE will come into picture. It will take more time to decide alternate path and configure the alternate path. Again there will be two flavours, (1) with router port + ZR and (2) others (ie. draft-ietf-teas-actn-poi-applicability) let's deep dive into the Fabio's slide in the next call. Also, I would like to join the discussions going forward and contribute to this standardization.

issues.title: Performance monitoring for IP domains
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body:
I think the scope of the draft will be both performance monitoring for IP and OP domains. In my opinion we shouldn't restrict to Optical only.

_Originally posted by @jbouqui153 in https://github.com/italobusi/draft-poidt-teas-actn-poi-assurance/pull/2#discussion_r1132439486_

issues.title: RCA for optical and IP/MPLS domains
issues.author: italobusi
issues.body:
here I think for fault/event detection in optical and IP/MPLS domain we need to have a wider scope. O/P-PNC start to implement alarm filtering capabilities joint with Root Cause Analysis and there the use of RFC8632 for exposing only the results of the RCA at P-PNC and O-PNC North Bound interfaces is not clear. In fact RFC8632 may not be the right data models for that purpose and this should be investigated. There is no real value to flood the MDSC with all Optical and IP/MPLS domains' alarms through the use of RFC8632

_Originally posted by @jbouqui153 in https://github.com/italobusi/draft-poidt-teas-actn-poi-assurance/pull/2#discussion_r1132448007_

pulls.title: First draft outline
pulls.author: italobusi
pulls.body: Prepare a first draft to submit to IETF 116

comments.author: prmanna
comments.body:
In general, assurance involves link failure, detect and re-route. Fabio’s slides depicts most those uses-cases in optical domain. There are PSM(protection switching module) modules can provide 50ms switchover in case of LOS. In rest of the cases, Optical PCE will come into picture. It will take more time to decide alternate path and configure the alternate path. 
Again there will be two flavours, (1) with router port + ZR and (2) others (ie. draft-ietf-teas-actn-poi-applicability) 
let's deep dive into the Fabio's slide in the next call.
Also, I would like to join the discussions going forward and contribute to this standardization.

comments.author: italobusi
comments.body:
> In general, assurance involves link failure, detect and re-route. Fabio’s slides depicts most those uses-cases in optical domain. There are PSM(protection switching module) modules can provide 50ms switchover in case of LOS. In rest of the cases, Optical PCE will come into picture. It will take more time to decide alternate path and configure the alternate path. Again there will be two flavours, (1) with router port + ZR and (2) others (ie. draft-ietf-teas-actn-poi-applicability) let's deep dive into the Fabio's slide in the next call. Also, I would like to join the discussions going forward and contribute to this standardization.

I have added some text to the draft based on your comment and referenced it in issue #1 so we can address it when reviewing the slides from Fabio

comments.author: Paolo-Volpato
comments.body:
As a proposal, I would introduce a new section between "YANG Data Models for the MPIs" and "Optical Network Failure and Performance Degradation".
The scope of this section could be to discuss the pre-requisites of the MDSC (and, in general, of the architecture) as listed by Fabio in his slides.
We can easily extend the pre-requisites and refer to them when discussing the specific cases.
An example of the section (title + text) follows.

Pre-requisites of MDSC in the cases discussed.
The following list summarizes the main pre-requisites of MDSC to handle the assurance cases described in the next sections.
Most of them have been already addressed in {{!I-D.ietf-teas-actn-poi-applicability}}

1. MDSC has acquired all the topology and status information of both the IP and optical layers.
2. MDSC is fully aware of any multi-layer connections between the IP and the optical layers. It is also
aware of the multi-domain interconnection links between different IP domains.
3. MDSC is aware of any topology or resource utilization change obtained in real time through coordination with the O/P-PNCs. This applies in the case of a fault or a maintenance activity involving either the IP or the DWDM layer.
4. MDSC coordinates the IP and DWDM protections and, as a result, the re-routing of traffic at both the IP
and DWDM layer. 
5. Before planned maintenance operation at the DWDM layer, MDSC instructs the P-PNC to move the affected IP traffic
hitlessly to another link. This is done before the event takes place. MDSC also coordinates with P-PNC to revert
back the traffic on the original path when the maintenance event is concluded.
6. When the O-PNC detects a degradation of optical performance (e.g. BER PRE-FEC values threshold crossing over
a certain period of time), it alerts the MDSC so that the MDSC relates the warning to an IP link. 
7. MDSC distinguishes between IP and Optical failures. For example, in the case of the failure of an IP port of a router, 
the IP traffic may be switched to a stand-by port, reusing the same ROADM optical resources (lambda, optical path) and keeping the end-to-end IP connection. If a remote IP node fails, then a re-route of optical resources takes place together with a switch of the local IP port in order to eatablish a new connection with a different IP node used for protection.

Perhaps we should make clear what a service is for us in the scope of POI: in the IP domain it is a L3VPN/L2VPN transport connectivity service from PE UNI to PE UNI (CPE is outside the scope). While E2E customer L3VPN/L2VPN service is from CPE to CPE. Then will be interesting in the draft that network assurance (IP link level, LSP level etc.) is managed by IP PNCs while in the draft we are focussing on the E2E L3VPN/L2VPN transport connectivity service with the underlying transport optical paths
A transport connectivity service can be requested either from MSC NBI (from OSS/orchestration layer) either directly from MDSC GUI
With a specific SLA and diversity constraints
not sure I understand the sentence "even if that is applied to the maintenance and protection cases"
I think the scope of the draft will be both performance monitoring for IP and OP domains. In my opinion we shouldn't restrict to Optical only.
There will be different cases here as operator may consider to implement protection only at IP/MPLS level and not at optical level for example

here I think for fault/event detection in optical and IP/MPLS domain we need to have a wider scope. O/P-PNC start to implement alarm filtering capabilities joint with Root Cause Analysis and there the use of RFC8632 for exposing only the results of the RCA at P-PNC and O-PNC North Bound interfaces is not clear. In fact RFC8632 may not be the right data models for that purpose and this should be investigated. There is no real value to flood the MDSC with all Optical and IP/MPLS domains' alarms through the use of RFC8632
Right, that sentence does add any informative value. I just wanted to say that we are "extending" the analysis to the cases described by Fabio. I will remove it from the main text.
I am having the same doubt. Shall we list all the possible options? This may extend the choices described in POI applicability, though.

reviews.author: FabioPeruzzini
reviews.body: Please clarify that failures within the IP network which do not require an interaction the optical network are out of scope of this document
